message:
test(pretty): Add insta snapshots for Pretty/Inspect renderables

Add snapshot test fixtures for the new Pretty and Inspect renderables:

src/renderables/snapshots/:
- pretty_wraps_to_width_and_is_stable.snap: Verifies width-aware wrapping
- inspect_shows_type_and_fields_when_available.snap: Verifies type info display

These snapshots lock down the Debug-based pretty printing output format,
ensuring consistent rendering across refactors.

Also sync beads issues.jsonl with current state:
- Close bd-1ohr: Pretty/Inspect implementation complete
- Update bd-1x5n: Theme work in progress

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:

 {"id":"bd-11gq","title":"Tree node markup parsing tests","description":"## Problem
Tree nodes may have similar markup parsing issues. Need explicit tests to verify and document behavior.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Test Tree node labels with markup - verify if parsed
- [ ] Test Tree with styled branches
- [ ] Verify ANSI codes present in rendered output (if markup should work)
- [ ] Verify raw markup tags NOT present in rendered output
- [ ] Document the expected API clearly

## Implementation Notes
- Check Tree implementation for markup handling
- Add explicit tests to lock down current behavior
- May need Tree::add_node_markup() if markup isn't supported

## Files to modify
- tests/conformance/tree_tests.rs  
- src/renderables/tree.rs (if fix needed)","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-25T04:59:52.753560616Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-25T08:00:16.526581194Z","closed_at":"2026-01-25T08:00:16.526523125Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["task"]}

-{"id":"bd-1ohr","title":"Port rich.pretty + rich.inspect equivalents","description":"Implement Rich-style pretty printing / inspection.

Scope:
- Provide a Pretty renderable for Rust values (Debug) with optional syntax highlighting
- Provide an inspect-like helper that prints type info + fields when possible

Acceptance criteria:
- Pretty output is stable and width-aware
- Unit tests cover nested structs/collections
- Document Rust-idiomatic differences vs Python inspect

 {"id":"bd-1ub","title":"Close Rich port gaps: parity, conformance, missing features","description":"Goal: close every remaining gap in the Python Rich -> rich_rust port by (1) establishing authoritative parity documentation, (2) adding true conformance tests against Python Rich output, and (3) implementing currently-missing features (Live, Layout, logging handler, HTML/SVG export) or explicitly documenting scope decisions.\n\nWhy: README still lists missing Rich features; spec/docs are out of sync with implemented functionality; conformance tests do not compare against Python Rich. This epic coordinates the remediation so future work does not rely on tribal knowledge.\n\nDefinition of done: FEATURE_PARITY.md exists and is authoritative; RICH_SPEC/README are consistent; conformance fixtures exist and are enforced; each missing feature is either implemented with tests/docs or explicitly excluded with rationale.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-25T00:24:50.961255334Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-25T10:26:09.960421969Z","closed_at":"2026-01-25T10:26:09.960404606Z","close_reason":"All parity docs, conformance harness, and missing features completed","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

Decide whether to implement or permanently exclude. Update FEATURE_PARITY.md, README, and RICH_SPEC accordingly.\n\nAcceptance: clear documented decision; if in-scope, follow-up beads created for spec/impl/tests.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-25T00:33:00.781151892Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-25T00:37:21.787501280Z","closed_at":"2026-01-25T00:37:21.787080357Z","close_reason":"Decided input widgets are out of scope and documented","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1ub.7","depends_on_id":"bd-1ub","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T17:35:49Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1vqq","title":"Fresh-eyes code exploration + bug audit pass (random modules)","description":"Goal: randomly sample core rendering modules, trace execution flows, and fix any obvious bugs found. Scope: Console/Segment/Text/Style pipeline and one renderable (Table/Panel/Progress). Method: read code paths end-to-end, cross-check invariants (width, newline handling, style transitions), then add targeted fixes/tests. Deliverables: bug fixes with tests or snapshot updates; notes in bead comments for findings.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-25T10:39:52.385060185Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-25T10:44:56.350960874Z","closed_at":"2026-01-25T10:44:56.350942369Z","close_reason":"Fix Columns padding overflow on narrow widths; add test; run fmt/check/clippy","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-1wk","title":"PrintOptions.soft_wrap has no behavioral effect","description":"PrintOptions::soft_wrap only forces width selection in Console::print_to; no soft-wrapping behavior is implemented in Text/Console rendering. Decide expected semantics and implement or remove flag.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-01-21T17:10:11.570072666Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-21T17:48:29.086509996Z","closed_at":"2026-01-21T17:48:29.086425517Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
-{"id":"bd-1x5n","title":"Port rich.theme: Theme + named styles (incl. .ini themes)","description":"Implement Python Rich theme support:

- Theme registry mapping style names -> Style
- Console has a theme, resolves named styles in markup and APIs
- Support loading theme from .ini (Python Rich Theme.read) to keep parity

Acceptance criteria:
- Unit tests for named style resolution + overrides
- Markup tag [warning] uses theme style when defined
- Conformance fixtures include a theme case
- Update docs/spec/parity

 {"id":"bd-1yc8","title":"Port rich.traceback + Console::print_exception equivalent","description":"Implement Python Rich traceback rendering and Console convenience API.

Scope:
- Render exceptions/backtraces to segments with syntax highlighting and locals formatting where possible
- Provide Console::print_exception (or Rust-idiomatic equivalent) matching Rich defaults
- Provide renderable Traceback type

Acceptance criteria:
- Deterministic output for a fixed synthetic stack in tests
- Optional feature flag integration if needed (e.g. backtrace)
- Conformance fixture compares against Python Rich for a controlled exception
","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-27T18:38:41.714278727Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T18:38:46.972281095Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1yc8","depends_on_id":"bd-3c90","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T18:38:46.972253544Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-21uy","title":"Panel markup parsing tests","description":"## Problem
Panel titles and content may have similar markup parsing issues as Table cells. Need explicit tests.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Test Panel::new() with markup in title - verify markup is parsed
- [ ] Test Panel with markup in content - verify markup is parsed  
- [ ] Test Panel with nested markup styles
- [ ] Verify ANSI codes present in rendered output
- [ ] Verify raw markup tags NOT present in rendered output

## Implementation Notes
- Check if Panel already parses markup correctly
- If not, may need to add Panel::from_markup() similar to Cell
- Add tests either way to document and lock down behavior

## Files to modify
- tests/conformance/panel_tests.rs
- src/renderables/panel.rs (if fix needed)","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-25T04:59:50.977751990Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-25T07:57:11.036711554Z","closed_at":"2026-01-25T07:57:11.036694292Z","close_reason":"Created tests/conformance/panel_tests.rs with 14 tests covering: Panel::from_text() does not parse markup, title/subtitle do not parse markup, pre-styled Text preserves styles via from_rich_text(), nested styles work correctly, ANSI codes present in styled output, raw markup tags absent in pre-styled content. Documents that Panel behavior is consistent with Cell (markup not auto-parsed).","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["task"]}
 {"id":"bd-24gn","title":"Progress bar description markup tests","description":"## Problem
Progress bar descriptions may have similar markup parsing issues. Need explicit tests.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Test Progress with markup in task description - verify if parsed
- [ ] Test Progress with markup in status text
- [ ] Verify expected behavior is documented
- [ ] Add markup support if missing and expected

## Files to modify
- tests/conformance/progress_tests.rs
- src/renderables/progress.rs (if fix needed)","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-25T04:59:54.496888342Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-25T15:22:18.842739933Z","closed_at":"2026-01-25T15:22:18.842679088Z","close_reason":"Progress markup conformance tests already present","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["task"]}
